100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 10, 1926, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin addressed the nation, emphasizing the need for unity in the face of economic challenges. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated as the government faced increasing pressure from both leftist and rightist factions. Across the Atlantic, American industry continued to thrive, with reports highlighting a surge in automobile production, signaling a robust post-war economy.
